title: Whats your thoughts on The N64 Evangelion video game
display_title: Whats your thoughts on The N64 Evangelion video game
wikitext:
[]: What's your thoughts on The N64 Evangelion video game             Quote Post by qazxswed1234567890 » Sat Dec 28, 2019 11:32 pm Personaly I'd say that it's fine for a while, but could have been better if they added some more angels and consistant gameplay, what's your thougts.

[]: Re: What's your thoughts on The N64 Evangelion video game             Quote Post by robersora » Sun Dec 29, 2019 10:42 pm I remember emulating this in like.... 2006 or something? I don't even know why I decided to do so, but this was my first and only foray into the world of emulators, lol. I think it's a fun idea, but the controls are horrible... Much more interested in playing 3nd impact, tbh.

[]: Re: What's your thoughts on The N64 Evangelion video game             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Mon Dec 30, 2019 12:55 am The game is really weird and has clunky controls. But I'd say it has its charme. Also, how great is it listening to the OST, with songs like Komm susser Tod sounding like they belong in Mario 64? And yes, 3nd Impact is awesome. I actually got a PSP this christmas and have been playing it.

[]: Re: What's your thoughts on The N64 Evangelion video game             Quote Post by Blockio » Tue Dec 31, 2019 9:11 am Which one are we talking about exactly? There are kinda a few of them. Not that I've actually played any of them, but I am a good bit into the lore of some of them

[]: Re: What's your thoughts on The N64 Evangelion video game             Quote Post by The Flying Fortress » Thu Jan 02, 2020 2:25 pm I think Bandai wisely took advantage of the movie's hype to launch a game and show off its graphics power on Nintendo 64, something more focused on fans and perhaps not on casual gamers of the time, the 2D-pseudo 3D perspective missions reminds me a little when I played Ultraman for SNES. The gameplay is as they said, the controls themselves do not follow a pattern throughout the game, with some missions, for example, I find the mission to defeat Israfael funny, if you miss the required commands, you will get a furious Asuka, Zeruel I really hated, usually "pressing this button in half a second" is something that discouraged me in this game. Just as most of the time battles are tied to the original story script limiting the EVA's movements, perhaps if there were more freedom for actions and different combat variables and in the story itself, some RPG concepts, as it was with NGE2, would have been more interesting.

Article: Whats your thoughts on The N64 Evangelion video game
 time, the 2D-pseudo 3D perspective missions reminds me a little when I played Ultraman for SNES. The gameplay is as they said, the controls themselves do not follow a pattern throughout the game, with some missions, for example, I find the mission to defeat Israfael funny, if you miss the required commands, you will get a furious Asuka, Zeruel I really hated, usually "pressing this button in half a second" is something that discouraged me in this game. Just as most of the time battles are tied to the original story script limiting the EVA's movements, perhaps if there were more freedom for actions and different combat variables and in the story itself, some RPG concepts, as it was with NGE2, would have been more interesting.
